definition: Passive investing (~55% of US equity AUM) has likely altered cycle dynamics through procyclical flow mechanics — price-insensitive flows mechanically reinforce concentration and create reflexivity loops. The Gabaix-Koijen inelastic markets hypothesis ($1 flow moves market cap by $3-5) amplifies the concern. However, no definitive passive-driven crash has occurred.
evidence: Passive share at ~55% of US equity AUM
Active managers still set marginal prices in many stocks
Five agents independently converge on this finding
No prior cycle with >25% passive share
Logical contradiction: cannot invoke historical base rates AND acknowledge they may be obsolete
risk_analyst_01 assigns 4/10 to magnitude calibration (appropriate for untested mechanism)
Every confidence rating depending on historical base rates should carry ±1 point uncertainty band
open_questions: Whether passive flows create their own returns in a non-equilibrium system
Whether a passive-driven crash is possible and what would trigger it
Whether every historical base rate for cycle behavior may be obsolete if hypothesis is correct
Most important open question for equity cycle research per synthesis
What is the formal protocol for adjusting base-rate-derived confidence ratings given 55% passive share?
Does passive investing preserve drawdown magnitude but distort velocity?
How does the Gabaix-Koijen multiplier interact with ETF creation/redemption during stress?
